item.title: China Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 82
item.description: The country’s deadliest mining disaster since 2009 left more than 100 workers injured and sparked a search for survivors.

item.title: China Is Throwing Christians in Jail, but This Pastor Refuses to Back Down
item.description: As Xi closed congregations around the country, Ezra Jin found ways to keep Zion Church going—and growing. It set him on a dangerous crash course with Beijing.

item.title: What Readers Found When They Asked Their Chatbots About China
item.description: Plus, a Hollywood screenwriter says historical disputes sank a U.S.-China film project.

item.title: The Former Air Force Pilot Who Was Allegedly Recruited to Train China’s Military
item.description: Combat veteran Gerald Eddie Brown was grounded in the U.S.—and looking for a chance to fly again.

item.title: China, U.S. Reach Limited Trade Agreements on Boeing Jets, Beef Imports
item.description: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing jets and resume imports of some U.S. beef products, marking one of the clearest signs yet of easing trade tensions between the two countries.

item.title: The Hidden Chinese Influence in AI
item.description: Plus, why AI grows more regime-friendly as press freedom declines.

item.title: Xi Jinping Is Now the World Leader He Wanted to Be, but It Has Come at a Cost
item.description: The Chinese leader’s policies have strained his country at home but given him great international stature.

item.title: China Eliminates Tariffs on Africa to Outmaneuver Trump
item.description: African countries are finding it harder to export to the U.S., while China sees it as an opportunity.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
